APIDescription: The Checksheets API is used to manage checksheet data.

Operations.Description:
Checksheets are the realization of control plan lines and are used to collect real-time inspection data. Control plans may be classified as either part or process. Part control plans are available at quality/{version}/control-plans, while process control recipes are available at quality/{version}/process-control-plans. Specifications are similarly divided into part and process, and can be found at quality/{version}/specifications and quality/{version}/process-specifications, respectively. There are two primary types of checksheets: live and non-live. A live checksheet is completed by an inspector directly within the Plex control panel. A non-live checksheet is filled out outside the control panel, such as through Checksheet launchers in Plex. The following guidelines outline the required information to create a checksheet:

ControlPlanId: Required for a non-live checksheet. If there are multiple process control recipes with the same PartId, OperationId, and InspectionMode, you must specify this.
ProductionWorkcenterId: Required for completing a live checksheet. The workcenter must be properly configured and have an operator logged in to Plex. The control plan must be designated as primary and must have an active control plan status ("Available For Use" flag set to true). Additionally, for part control plans, the Inspection Mode Manufacturing flag must be set to true, and the selected part operation must be active and not designated as a sub-operation. For process control recipes, the Inspection Mode Process Check flag must be set to true, the control plan type must have its process flag enabled, and it must belong to a process control recipe family.
ApprovedWorkcenterId: Relevant only for a non-live checksheet. For a part and operation, these are available in production/{version}/production-definitions/approved-workcenters
InspectorEmployeeId: Do not specify this if you are providing the ProductionWorkcenterId. For a live checksheet, this field is automatically set to the primary operator logged into the workcenter. If no operator is specified, the inspector defaults to the Plex API user. Employee information is available at mdm/{version}/employees.
JobOperationId: In live mode, if sent, should match the setup of the control panel. They can be found at scheduling/{version}/jobs.
PartOperationId: In live mode, if sent, should match the setup of the control panel. They can be found at mdm/{version}/part-operations. If ProductionWorkcenterId is specified, it is optional to send the PartId, PartOperationId, JobOperationId, and ControlPlanId. If JobOperationId is specified, it is optional to send the PartId and PartOperationId. Process control recipe validations differ slightly from those of part control plans. For part control plans, you can add the checksheet to either a live or non-live checksheet. For process control recipes, the configuration of the process control recipe family, along with the "Control Panel" group and the "All Process Checksheets Display" setting, determines whether the submitted checksheet is valid. If the recipe family is configured as workcenter-only, the checksheet may be added only in the live-mode to any part associated with the specified ProductionWorkcenterId, provided the operation matches that of the recipe family. If the recipe family is set up with both parts and workcenters, the checksheet can only be added to those specific combinations of parts, workcenters, and operations defined in the recipe family. If the "All Process Checksheets Display" setting is enabled, the restrictions for workcenter-only or part-specific assignments no longer apply. In this case, as long as the ProductionWorkcenterId and Operation are aligned, the checksheet may be added to any part only in the live-mode.

Sample Request for a Live Checksheet

{
  "productionWorkcenterId": "AF05A9CC-83D6-4A95-A79D-0932906A7212",
  "operationId": "9C10C43A-4181-40CF-BE62-4B5ED0034F0D",
  "inspectionMode": "In Process (each container)",
  "measurements": [
    {
      "specificationId": "168E2C69-31AB-43B2-9B61-BC6A22BA0719",
      "value": 1
    }
  ]
}
            


Sample Request for a Non-Live Checksheet

{
  "controlPlanId": "616f5aad-0d68-48be-af5a-39da2f932f9d",
  "approvedWorkcenterId": "57DFB705-7122-431D-B5B7-246DF6130BE7",
  "partId": "85989389-5CE1-4FDD-95D9-94A3CD663CE6",
  "operationId": "3C85A9B0-3B87-4FF5-95A6-1709D45986CE",
  "partOperationId": "a0a315d1-ef64-4e74-9d56-02b430ea5e1f",
  "inspectionMode": "Process Checks - Setup",
  "measurements": [
    {
      "specificationId": "497ABAB9-D86D-4674-99F6-9586ED8D1578",
      "value": 510
    }
  ]
}



Unsupported Functionality:

Streams
Piece Measurements

This operation supports sending in a maximum of 100 specifications for a checksheet.

RequestParameters.description: A unique identifier representing the Control Plan.
A unique identifier representing the Workcenter. This will usually represent a live checksheet.

SPC Checksheet Container Text.

A unique identifier representing the Specification.

The Measurement Value.
  Attribute specifications are qualitative part features that can only result in a count of non-conformances, 
  like the presence of a burrs on a part, leak or no leak etc.
  You may collect attribute data against a variable spec by specifying the pass / fail condition on the control plan line form, 
  so if a specification can be measured on a continuous scale, then enter it as a variable spec.
  Measurements for attribute type must specify either a 1 or a 0 signifying a pass or a fail scenario. 


  Variable specifications are something that is not an attribute specification. 
  It can be measured on a continuous scale, such as height, weight, diameter, temperature, etc.
  These would be tolerance types such as:
  Two Sided (10 mm +/- 0.05 mm)
  One Sided Maximum (10 mm max)
  Based on which tolerance type you select, the limits will be calculated. For example, for a Two Sided Tolerance, 
  fields for the target and +/- values will be needed.


  A specification could have one or more bonus tolerances associated with it.
  An example for when this is needed: You have a feature size between 10mm to 11mm or 10.5mm +/- .5mm. 
  In addition there is an allowable maximum material condition of .02 or bonus tolerance for straightness.
  If the feature size was at it's maximum say 11mm, then the bonus tolerance would be .02mm.
  The way this will be need to be defined in part specification would be as below :
  - Create a Part Specification for the Feature Size of 10.5mm +/- .5mm.
  - Create another Part Specification for the Bonus Tolerance. In this example we are using a Tolerance Type "One-Sided Maximum" and added the .02mm.
  - Specify a Bonus Tolerance and add a Part Specification with the feature size of 10.5mm as the Dependent Specification. 
  - Make the Applied To as "Upper" and the Bonus Side as "Upper".
  Finally create a Control Plan using these two Part Specifications. When you fillout a checksheet and add a dimenstion for the feature size, 
  the bonus specification will calculate in the target area automatically and you can enter a measurement as long as it meets the allowed target.
  Note: The setting "Bonus Tolerance When Out of Spec Apply" will also contribute to the upper and lower bonus tolerance.
  When turned on, bonus tolerance values will be calculated even when the bonus is based on a measurement that is out-of-spec. 
  When off, no bonus is applied from out-of-spec measurements.


  Compare value specifications only apply to attribute Specifications. 
  It works in conjunction with the Control Plan line if you turn on the setting "Custom Checksheet Data Collection Type Display". 
  For specifying such compare value functions add the value on the note section of measurements.
The Measurement Values.
The note on the Measurement Group.

The Gage ID for the Measurement Group. Note this will be saved on the checksheet only if the Setting Control_Plans , Gage ID Store is set to true. This field will be required if the corresponding inspection mode requires it.

APIDescription: You can use this to query the data points(numerical tags data) and events(boolean and string tags data) from Plex APM. In order to query the data, currently, only $filter, $select and $top query parameters are supported. How to use the API: Following are sample OData parameters queries (Query options overview - OData | Microsoft Docs) $select: For retrieving specific columns in the response, you should provide $select parameter. Following are the acceptable values for select query: $top: For limiting response to a specific record count, you should provide $top parameter. If user does not provide $top parameter, response will be served with a default limit of 50000. $top accepts a non-negative integer value. $filter: For fetching the tag data, you must provide $filter parameter. Allowed logical operators for filter query are - eq, and, in, startswith, endswith. Following list describes allowed $filter query parameters, along with their acceptable values/format: Following are examples of filter, select, and top query that combine logical operator and query parameters.

Operations.Description: Returns data points(numerical tags data) and events(boolean and string tags data) on which you can add filter and select query. Endpoint is rate limited to serve 40K requests per day.

RequestParameters.name: $select

RequestParameters.name: $top

APIDescription: The Purchase Orders API is used to retrieve purchase order information, including line items. This API exposes endpoints to retrieve purchase orders and their sub-resources in the context of Purchasing. Use a GET request to retrieve the information about purchase orders, line items related to purchase orders, purchase order types, and statuses.

Operations.Description: Retrieve a PO line item by ID. A line item on a purchase order contains information that specifies the purchased item or service along with the low-level details of the order, including the price (depending on configuration) and release schedule. Most of the fields available in a line item are controlled by the PO type. Some fields are also controlled by module settings and setup table configuration.

Operations.Description: Retrieve a purchase order by ID. Purchase orders (POs) are created for single-use "spot buys" or recurring blanket orders of goods from suppliers. For example, a component part must appear on the Parts screen to order it from a supplier. Once the purchase order is created in the system, Get Purchase Order is used to retrieve the purchase order information.

URLParameters.description: A unique identifier for the purchase order.
